title: Post Death Kaji included in EoEs Instrumentality
display_title: Post Death Kaji included in EoEs Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Post-Death Kaji included in EoE's Instrumentality?             Quote Post by obliterates » Fri Apr 26, 2013 2:23 pm This is just an interesting thought I noticed while rewatching End of Evangelion. I don't know if this was brought up before, and if it was, a point I'm going to make was not introduced in the discussion. About one hour into EoE, Shinji watches Kaji and Misato having sex in college, which is a projection of Misato's memories (presumably). This scene appears in the EoTV narrative as well. We all know that the Kaji and Misato we hear in this scene are younger. Misato's meta-commentary ("Yes, I also do this" etc) we can assume is the Misato during 3I Instrumentality. It would seem that the Kaji presented in NGE doesn't actually appear anywhere in the movie. But I've taken these quick screencaps that appear to show otherwise: This scene is only a few frames long and barely a second in length, right after Asuka asks if she will be doing 'the same kind of stuff' Misato does when she grows up. At first glance, I thought this was a continuation of Misato's college memories, but at further inspection I noticed that this Kaji doesn't resemble the college Kaji at all. For starters, a young Kaji had short(er) hair that was left down. And his physical build doesn't suggest late teenage or early twenties. And of course, we see stubble in these frames, which is indicated above. It's pretty obvious what this scene is showing. What's interesting is the stagelight that surfaces right before Kaji "rises": is very reminiscent of the stagelights in Shinji's sandbox scene, (which occurred only 3 minutes before). What does this mean? Is this a post-Death Kaji? A Episode 20 Kaji from Misato's memories? Something else entirely? If we can conclude from Misato's commentary that dead people's 'souls' can be included in Instrumentality, then maybe we can apply the same thinking in this scene. Is this Kaji's Instrumentality?

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri Apr 26, 2013 2:59 pm I had always took it as just Misato's memory in EoE, but I suppose Kaji himself also appears in Instrumentality in EoTV (During the "Congratulations!" scene at least) so...I dunno.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Apr 26, 2013 5:57 pm Kaji and Shinji are part of the overall dialog during EoTV: Episode 26 wrote: Kaji (PHONE - OFF): Do you hate feeling lonely? Shinji (OFF): I don't like it. Kaji (PHONE - OFF): Do you hate suffering? ---- Kaji (OFF): The Eva itself will become all you are. Misato: The real you will cease to exist. Shinji (OFF): I don't care! I never had anything to begin with anyway! Even the cello lessons I used to take all came to nothing. ---- Kaji: That's about how solid the truth is for humans. Though, that's what also makes people seek a deeper truth. IKARI: You are merely not used to being loved by other people. Misato: So there's no need for you to constantly care about what other people think of you. Shinji: But doesn't everybody hate me? Asuka: What are you, stupid?! You're just making yourself believe that, that's all! Shinji: But I hate myself.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Apr 26, 2013 6:19 pm Kaji is part of EoTV's instrumentality, but it's up in the air as to if he's in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 26, 2013 6:57 pm The Killer of Heroes wrote: I had always took it as just Misato's memory in EoE

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri Apr 26, 2013 7:18 pm Is that a common argument that's brought up against Concurrency?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 26, 2013 7:23 pm One of them, yes. Same goes for Toji, Kensuke and Hikari, as well as the bridge bunnies, Fuyutsuki and Gendo appearing within instrumentality. On the other hand, Kaworu doesn't show up at all in the EoTV version. Plenty of threads around for concurrency arguments though, no need to start a new one.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri Apr 26, 2013 7:25 pm Wasn't really interested in debating it, but it was just something I had never really considered before.

[]: Re: Post-Death Kaji included in EoE's Instrumentality?             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Apr 26, 2013 8:19 pm obliterates wrote: It's pretty obvious what this scene is showing. What's interesting is the stagelight that surfaces right before Kaji "rises": is very reminiscent of the stagelights in Shinji's sandbox scene, (which occurred only 3 minutes before). What does this mean? Is this a post-Death Kaji? A Episode 20 Kaji from Misato's memories? Something else entirely?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sat Apr 27, 2013 8:16 am I might be saying something embarassingly stupid, but i think some old discussion threads linked the lights in P3II to either Yui or some sort of godly observer (something like the god-like third point of view narrator in a novel)? Seeing that, as OMF mentioned, memory and reality become fused during Instrumentality, i would initially assume that to be a symbolic cue/hint that everyone's memories and thoughts are being interepreted and "copied" towards some central being, possibly Lilith. The overhead light cast over them might also intend to inspire a feeling of judgement upon humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Apr 27, 2013 10:18 am Dream wrote: The overhead light cast over them might also intend to inspire a feeling of judgement upon humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Wed May 08, 2013 10:24 am Theoretically, and I'm probably way off on how Instrumentality works, but the idea is that all the different versions of yourself come together. So the version of yourself you think you are and the version your friends and family think you are all must come together and form one new version of yourself. And what we see is Shinji coming to terms with the different versions of himself. So Kaji should exist, just without the version of how he sees himself, because he is dead. So the Instrumentality Kaji would consist of Misato-Kaji, Shinji-Kaji, Asuka-Kaji, etc. He'd probably be a little unstable actually, without his original self I would think.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed May 08, 2013 12:05 pm ...that's an interesting thought.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed May 08, 2013 5:11 pm Lonecow12 wrote: Theoretically, and I'm probably way off on how Instrumentality works, but the idea is that all the different versions of yourself come together. So the version of yourself you think you are and the version your friends and family think you are all must come together and form one new version of yourself. And what we see is Shinji coming to terms with the different versions of himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 08, 2013 5:22 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Actually I believe that's exactly how Instrumentality is said to work in EoTV. It would disappoint a lot of Kaji fans, but this would actually make more sense than Kaji actually being in Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed May 08, 2013 8:43 pm Monk Ed wrote: Why?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 08, 2013 9:18 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Because Kaji died long before Instrumentality began. It requires us to postulate that his soul "hung around" in Hakone long enough to still be a part of Instrumentality at all. This is particularly difficult when we have the Rei's explicitly "collecting" Misato and Ritsuko in EoE. There's no matching scene for Kaji.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu May 09, 2013 7:47 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Because Kaji died long before Instrumentality began. It requires us to postulate that his soul "hung around" in Hakone long enough to still be a part of Instrumentality at all. This is particularly difficult when we have the Rei's explicitly "collecting" Misato and Ritsuko in EoE. There's no matching scene for Kaji.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu May 09, 2013 8:42 am Bagheera wrote: We do have an explicit scene of such in the manga (different continuity, I know, but still) and we have no particular reason to think souls go anywhere of theri own accord unless they're collected. We also know they can be collected from bodies that have been dead for awhile, as we see when Lilith is collecting souls from the dead bodies throughout the geofront. Personally, I think it's likely souls stay with the body as long as it exists. Once it decays the soul presumably decays with it and is recycled like ordinary matter, but until then it just stays put. Why? No idea. But given how things seem to work in EoE that's what we're left with.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu May 09, 2013 9:03 am xanderkh wrote: Wow, that's a pretty depressing view of death. Hopefully there's more to this in this universe than what we're shown in EoE.
